title: Real-image robustness stress test for the star identifier
description: Validate that the existing C identifier (TETRA + Pyramid) and centroid pipeline still work on real night-sky images from multiple survey sources, across FOV 8-15 deg (same generated database), and under added noise and satellite streaking. No changes to the identifier DB or algorithm constants.

stories.title: Multi-source fetch + pipeline works on >=2 distinct survey sources
acceptanceCriteria: A reusable test script exists under scripts/ that can fetch a night-sky field from at least 2 distinct survey sources (e.g. DSS2 Red plus 2MASS-J and/or Mellinger Red) via astroquery/SkyView.
Fetched raw images are cached locally so re-runs do not re-download.
For each source, the full pipeline (centroid_extract.exe -> demo_centroid_compare.exe) runs to completion and the script parses detected-star count plus TETRA/Pyramid success + RA/DEC.
Measured accuracy (angular error vs known field center, tolerance 0.5 deg) is reported per source for at least 4 fields, and the numbers are recorded in progress.txt.

stories.title: FOV 8-15 deg sweep using the same database
acceptanceCriteria: The script sweeps at least FOV = 8, 10, 12, 15 deg on real images of the same field centers, passing the FOV through to demo_centroid_compare.exe without rebuilding/regenerating the database.
Per-FOV TETRA and Pyramid accuracy (within 0.5 deg of truth) is reported over >=4 fields each.
Results recorded in progress.txt, noting any FOV at which accuracy degrades and a brief explanation.

stories.title: Noise + satellite streaking robustness
acceptanceCriteria: The script can add Gaussian noise at multiple sigma levels and inject synthetic satellite streaks (simulating a moving satellite) into real DSS images before centroiding.
Pipeline accuracy is measured under each noise level and each streak count over >=4 fields.
Results recorded in progress.txt, identifying the noise/streak level at which identification starts to fail.
